name: NUMT_genomewide_dispersed
description: Dispersed pattern of >700 nuclear mitochondrial DNA insertions (NUMTs) scattered across all autosomes and X, ranging from ~100 bp to ~14 kb at 80-99% identity to chrM. Reads from chrM commonly mismap to NUMTs and vice versa, generating spurious heteroplasmy calls. This entry is a pattern/landscape marker, not a single coordinate.

coordinates.note: pattern, not a single contiguous coordinate range

architecture.internal_structure: Hundreds of NUMTs distributed across all nuclear chromosomes; sizes from <100 bp to ~14 kb; identities to chrM 80-99% (older NUMTs more diverged); largest single NUMTs on chr1, chr5 (chr5q33), chr11.

related_diseases: False mtDNA heteroplasmy in clinical sequencing
